YouQuestions answered: - use_case: All of the above — need both dark + light variants - content_fidelity: Same stages, tighten the copy - variations: 2–3 distinct directions - directions: Decide for me - accent_use: Moderate — green for the active/current stage - animation: Static SVG + optional animated HTML variant - format: SVG file only (drop-in replacement) - tweaks: Decide for me
Claude

Got it. Let me plan this out:

  • 2–3 distinct directions, each as a drop-in SVG file
  • Dark + light variants
  • Moderate green accent (one "live" stage)
  • Static SVGs + an HTML showcase page for side-by-side review with tweaks

Let me pick the directions:

  1. Graphite cards — cleanest, most literal rebrand of the current diagram (the safest drop-in)
  2. Terminal/pipe flow — monospace, box-drawing, feels like | pipe | chain | — very on-brand for ZettelForge's terminal aesthetic
  3. Neural signal flow — nodes + edges echoing the shield's neuron motif, more distinctive

Let me build these.

Progress 0/5

Let me draft the first direction — Graphite cards. This is the closest rebrand of the current diagram: stacked full-width cards, graphite surfaces, #30363D borders, fog type, one green pulse on the "active" stage, with Inter + JetBrains Mono.

Copy tightening (same stages):

  • Raw input → reports · alerts · notes · IOCs
    1. Ingestion → validate → extract entities → resolve aliases / regex fast-path · LLM fallback
    1. Enrichment → causal triples · neighbor refinement · HGAM consolidation
    1. Retrieval → intent classify · vector + graph · temporal boost · rerank
    1. Synthesis → RAG-as-answer · quality validated · grounded to sources
  • Storage: SQLite (default) LanceDB
  • Extensions: TypeDB STIX 2.1 · OAuth/JWT · OpenCTI · Sigma
Writing

Let me preview that first one to check the layout.

graphite-dark.svg

Let me just build everything, then verify at the end via the showcase page.

Now let me build the light variant of graphite cards, then direction 2 (terminal) and direction 3 (neural).

Writing

Now direction 2 — Terminal/pipe. This uses monospace everywhere, box-drawing characters for the pipeline, a shell-prompt feel. The "live" stage gets a [ ● LIVE ] tag. It's the most on-brand direction (README terminal aesthetic).

Writing

I realized I didn't need to preview — the terminal diagram was included. Note: the terminal.svg has a "Storage / Extensions" block missing; I need to either add them inline or scope this SVG to just the pipeline. Since it's meant to be a drop-in replacement of zettelforge_architecture.svg which includes storage + extensions, I need to add those. Let me extend the terminal SVG and build its light variant, plus the neural direction.

Editing, Reading

The style element got stripped somehow — the SVG got parsed and re-serialized on disk without <style>. Let me just rewrite the terminal SVG cleanly with all content (pipeline + storage + extensions) and proper style tags.

Writing

Let me script the remaining files (terminal-light, neural dark+light, showcase) to be efficient.

Running script

Now I'll build direction 3 — Neural signal flow. This echoes the shield's neuron motif: four circular "cell body" nodes connected by dashed recall-trail paths (matching the logo's axon), with dendrite sparkles on the live node.

Fork verifier agent

Three directions, each as dark + light SVG, plus a showcase page with Tweaks for switching between them:

  • svg/graphite-dark.svg · svg/graphite-light.svg — closest rebrand of the current diagram
  • svg/terminal-dark.svg · svg/terminal-light.svg — monospace terminal/pipeline feel, most on-brand for ZettelForge
  • svg/neural-dark.svg · svg/neural-light.svg — nodes-on-an-axon echo of the shield's neuron motif
  • architecture.html — side-by-side viewer; toggle Tweaks to switch direction / theme

Retrieval is the default LIVE stage (moderate accent: one green pulse). Copy tightened to real class names (MemoryManager, BlendedRetriever, ContextInjector) and real measurements (~45ms, 66.9% LOCOMO, +33%).

Caveat: the "live stage" tweak in the panel is a UI scaffold — to actually move the LIVE indicator to a different stage in the SVGs themselves, each stage would need an id and a small class-swap script (or six more SVG variants). Say the word and I'll wire that up. Also happy to pick one direction and polish it further.
